_README: CLASSICAL-CONFORMANCE CORPUS. Each entry is a documented horary case whose date/time/place/question/verdict is SOURCED (see `source` + verbatim `sourceQuote`). `lillyVerdict` is the practitioner's own stated verdict; `expectedLean` maps it to Kairos's lean vocabulary; `kairosLean` PINS Kairos's CURRENT live lean for this chart (regression protection, like horary-golden.test.ts). `inScope=false` marks a KNOWN GAP (a chart that uses judgment Kairos does not yet model — turning the chart, natural significators, directional/where reasoning, mundane multi-house synthesis), NOT a bug. DATA INTEGRITY: do not add a case you cannot source with a date, place and the author's stated verdict. Lilly times reproduced from modern recalculations are LMT for London (IANA Europe/London resolves to London Mean Time for these pre-1847 dates).

cases.source: William Lilly, Christian Astrology (1647), Book III, p. 385 ('A Lady, if marry the Gentleman desired?'); chart data per Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2012-09-30 and 2024-05-02.
cases.sourceQuote: the Match effected, and all within twenty days following, to the content of the sorrowful (but as to me unthankful) Lady.
cases.question: A lady asks whether she shall marry the gentleman she desires.
cases.note: Lilly first leaned NO on a void Moon, then re-judged YES via the Moon's translation of light Mercury->Jupiter and a Jupiter-Saturn link; the marriage occurred within 20 days. Kairos sees the void Moon and the separating significators and leans unfavorable -- a translation/denial-weighting gap, exactly the kind of disagreement a future change should resolve.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'relationshipexample.html' ('Where is this Relationship Going?').
cases.sourceQuote: The relationship is not going anywhere, at least not in a positive way ... the querent reported to me that she had the predicted difficulties with the relationship.
cases.question: Where is this relationship going? (Will the relationship prosper?)
cases.note: Warnock read deterioration (boyfriend drifting to another); outcome confirmed. Kairos sees a translation carrier (Jupiter) but a void Moon nets it to a near-zero uncertain -- a denial/translation-weighting gap.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'jobexample.html' ('Will I Get a Job in 90 Days?').
cases.sourceQuote: it is highly doubtful that I will get a job in the time specified ... Not surprisingly I didn't get a job.
cases.question: Will I get a job within 90 days?
cases.note: Warnock used the 6th house (employment/service) and a weak late-Capricorn Moon to judge NO; no job followed. Kairos finds a Venus carrier and a void Moon, netting to uncertain rather than unfavorable.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'hegotjobexample.html' ('He Got the Job!').
cases.sourceQuote: The presence of Saturn, 10th ruler dignified, in the 1st, indicates that the querent will get the job ... The querent quickly obtained a new job.
cases.question: Will I get the job?
cases.note: Warnock judged YES from the dignified 10th ruler (Saturn) placed in the 1st -- a 'significator in the relevant house' testimony Kairos does not weight. Kairos finds a Mercury carrier but a void Moon and lands uncertain. Outcome was YES.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'gradschooladmissionexample.html' ('Will I get into my chosen graduate school?').
cases.sourceQuote: decidedly negative ... The querent was not admitted to graduate school.
cases.question: Will I be admitted to my chosen graduate school?
cases.note: Weak querent significator separating from the 9th; not admitted. Kairos also leans unfavorable. Agreement.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'whenwillwesellourhouseexample.html' ('When Will Our House Sell?').
cases.sourceQuote: Saturn ... retrograde and in detriment ... delays and difficulties ... four months after this question, the querent was unable to sell his house.
cases.question: When will our house sell? (Will the house sell?)
cases.note: Retrograde, detrimented buyer-significator and afflicted price ruler; no sale within four months. Kairos also leans unfavorable (high). Agreement.

cases.source: Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'testingexample.html' ('Will I pass my finals and graduate from college?').
cases.sourceQuote: the querent will indeed pass their final exams and graduate ... The querent passed all exams and graduated from college, doing even better than expected!
cases.question: Will I pass my finals and graduate from college?
cases.note: Mercury (querent) applies to a conjunction with dignified Venus (the college/test) in the 9th; passed. Kairos also leans favorable via direct perfection. Agreement. Source page annotates the chart "GMT -3"; the astronomically correct offset for Tel Aviv in June 2003 is UTC+3 (Asia/Jerusalem, used here) — the page notation is a quirk, the chart data matches.

cases.source: Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2011-12-12 ('Pregnant in Athens? A Horary Pregnant with Possibilities').
cases.sourceQuote: the woman was not pregnant; and she ended up needing surgery to remove a cyst that was making her unable to conceive.
cases.question: Am I pregnant?
cases.note: Outcome: not pregnant. Kairos also leans unfavorable. Agreement. (Real, resolved outcome -- a candidate for the later calibration corpus once enough resolved cases exist.)

cases.source: Anthony Louis, tonylouis.wordpress.com 2017-10-27 ('An interesting horary chart' -- Will he pass the exam?).
cases.sourceQuote: The Moon ... had just enough strength by accidental fortitudes to transfer the light from Jupiter to Mercury ... The student passed with the minimum passing grade.
cases.question: Will he pass the exam?
cases.note: A narrow YES (minimum passing grade) carried by the Moon translating light Jupiter->Mercury despite the Moon being in detriment. Kairos weights the debilitated Moon negatively and leans unfavorable -- a translation-by-a-weak-Moon gap. Practitioner verdict qualified-but-affirmative -> expectedLean favorable.

cases.source: William Lilly, Christian Astrology (1647), Book III, pp. 212-214 ('Whether the Querent should follow the King or stay with Parliament'); chart data per Christopher Warnock, renaissanceastrology.com 'horarylilly1644.html' (13 April 1644, 8:55 am, London).
cases.sourceQuote: his Majesties designes were betrayed and his cause sinking; and his own losses would be infinite great, even to his utter impoverishment, if he declined not his present resolution.
cases.question: Should the querent leave Parliament's London to follow King Charles at Oxford?
cases.gapReason: Mundane decision judged on NATURAL SIGNIFICATORS and multi-house synthesis (Sun for the King, the King's prospects across the 10th/11th, the querent's estate), not a single quesited-house perfection. The 9th-house mapping here is a rough proxy; Kairos's single-significator perfection model cannot reproduce Lilly's reasoning.
cases.note: Lilly advised against following the King ('do not go to Oxford'); the querent ignored it and lost an estate, confirming Lilly. Logged as a known gap, not a bug.
